I've been waiting for this day for what feels like an eternity! Tim Burton's Beetlejuice Beetlejuice is finally here, and it's everything I've hoped for and more. The original film was a masterpiece of gothic comedy, and this sequel manages to capture that same magic while also adding a fresh layer of humor and heart. Michael Keaton is as brilliant as ever as Beetlejuice, the titular demon with a penchant for mischief and mayhem. His chemistry with Winona Ryder is as strong as ever, and it's a delight to see them back together on screen. The new cast members, including Justin Theroux, Monica Bellucci, and Jenna Ortega, fit seamlessly into the world of Beetlejuice.
The film's visuals are stunning, and Burton's signature style is on full display. The costumes, sets, and special effects are all top-notch, and they help to create a truly immersive experience. Danny Elfman's score is perfect, and it adds to the film's eerie and atmospheric feel.
But what really sets Beetlejuice Beetlejuice apart is its heart. It's a film about family, loss, and the power of love. It's a film that will make you laugh, cry, and think. It's a film that will stay with you long after the credits roll.
If you're a fan of the original Beetlejuice or just a fan of great filmmaking, I highly recommend checking out this sequel. It's a must-see.

Quit snatching all the old, good movies and crapping them up with remaking/rebooting/extending just so you money grubbers can keep stuffing your bank accounts!!"  For some time now it seems like nobody makes stand-alone movies anymore: they HAVE TO make a trilogy at least, and too many try to mimic Harry Potter or other long franchises and make 6 or 7.

After Hocus Pocus 2 this has been even more of my natural reaction when I realized even movies that were meant to have everything encompassed in one sitting weren't safe anymore, and this is far from the only movie like this.  Like one person said: "...the studios resorting to dusting off old time classics and changing them up/adding more to them just to keep the money flowing."  Especially stand-alone movies; they were as good as they were because they were singular.  They put everything necessary into the movie to enjoy the world of it to the fullest and didn't stretch it out to make any of it go stale or boring.

I have to admit though, this one was pretty decent.  I'll definitely want to check this one out.  If they do it right (and this is what too many studios don't seem to give a sh- - about as long as the $$$ comes in) and the movie is as good as this trailer, then I think this will be a much needed and appreciated diamond in the rough in the sea of soulless reskinning and stretching out.
